H719.def: § Arvad = "I shall break loose" a city-island near Sidon

H720.def: A man of the tribe of Gad living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Gen.46.16; son of: Gad (H1410); brother of: Ziphion (H6837), Haggi (H2291), Shuni (H7764), Ezbon (H0675), Eri (H6179) and Areli (H0692); also called Arod at Num.26.17; Also named: a.ro.di (אֲרוֹדִי "Arodi" H0722) § Arod = "I shall subdue: I shall roam" a son of Gad

H721.def: Someone descended from Arvad(?) of the Canaanites, first mentioned at Gen.10.18; descended from Canaan (H3667); along with Sidon (H6721), Heth (H2845), Jebusite (H2983), Amorite (H0567), Girgashite (H1622), Hivite (H2340), Arkite (H6208), Sinite (H5513), Zemarite (H6786) and Hamathite (H2577) § Arvadites = "I shall break loose" the descendants of Arvad, a son of Canaan

H722.def: Someone descended from Arod, a man of the tribe of Gad living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Gen.46.16; son of: Gad (H1410); brother of: Ziphion (H6837), Haggi (H2291), Shuni (H7764), Ezbon (H0675), Eri (H6179) and Areli (H0692); also called Arod at Num.26.17; Another name of a.rod (אֲרוֹד "Arod" H0720) § Arodi or Arodites = "I shall subdue: I shall roam" descendants of Arod

H723.def: manger, crib, stall (for animals) Also means: a.ve.rah (אֲוֵרָה "stall" H0220)

H725.def: Arumah = "I shall be exalted" a place near Shechem, home of Abimelech Also named: ru.mah (רוּמָה "Rumah" H7316H)

H726.def: Someone from Aram, an Aramite, Syrian (Kethib), Edomite Group of e.dom (אֱדוֹם "Edom" H0123)

H727.def: 1) chest, ark 1a) money chest 1b) Ark of the Covenant 2)(TWOT) coffin

H728.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.24.16; also called Araunah at 2Sa.24.16,18,20,21,22,23,24; Another name of or.nan (אׇרְנָן "Ornan" H0771) § Araunah = "I shall shout for joy" = "make ye to shine" (2Sa 24:16) or "joyful shouting of Jah" (2Sa 24:18) a Jebusite who sold David the site for an altar

H729.def: (CLBL) v 1) (CLBL) to be firm 2) (CLBL) made firm (past part.) adj 3) (BDB/TWOT) firm, strong

H730.def: 1) cedar 1a) cedar tree 1b) cedar timber, cedar wood (in building) 1c) cedar wood (in purifications)

H732.def: 1) to wander, journey, go, keep company with 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to journey, go 1a2) (part.) 1a2a) wandering, wayfarer, journeying 1a3) (subst.) 1a3a) wanderer, wayfarer, traveller

H733.def: A man of the tribe of Asher living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.7.39; son of: Shual (H7777B); brother of: Hanniel (H2592H) and Rizia (H7525) § Arah = "traveller" 1) a man of Asher 2) the prominent head of a family of returning exiles 3) another returned exile

H734.def: 1) way, path 1a) path, road 1b) the path, way, passing of life (fig.) 1c) way of living (fig.) 1d) traveller, wayfarer (meton) Aramaic equivalent: o.rach (אֹ֫רַח "way" H0735)

H735.def: way, path, course of life Aramaic of o.rach (אֹ֫רַח "way" H0734)

H739.def: A group living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.23.20 meaning uncertain 1a) (CLBL) possibly lion-like 1b) (BDB) possibly two sons of Ariel of Moab

H740.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Ezr.8.16 § Ariel = "lion of God" or "lioness of God" 1) a name applied to Jerusalem 2) the name of a chief of the returning exiles

H742.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Est.9.9; son of: Haman (H2001); brother of: Parshandatha (H6577), Dalphon (H1813), Aspatha (H0630), Poratha (H6334), Adalia (H0118), Aridatha (H0743), Parmashta (H6534), Arisai (H0747) and Vaizatha (H2055) § Aridai = "the lion is enough" a son of Haman

H743.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Est.9.8; son of: Haman (H2001); brother of: Parshandatha (H6577), Dalphon (H1813), Aspatha (H0630), Poratha (H6334), Adalia (H0118), Parmashta (H6534), Arisai (H0747), Aridai (H0742) and Vaizatha (H2055) § Aridatha = "the lion of the decree" a son of Haman

H745.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 2Ki.15.25 § Arieh = "lion" an officer of king Pekahiah

H747.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Est.9.9; son of: Haman (H2001); brother of: Parshandatha (H6577), Dalphon (H1813), Aspatha (H0630), Poratha (H6334), Adalia (H0118), Aridatha (H0743), Parmashta (H6534), Aridai (H0742) and Vaizatha (H2055) § Arisai = "lion of my banners (?)" a son of Haman

H748.def: 1) to be long, prolong 1a) (Qal) to be long 1b) (Hiphil) 1b1) to prolong (days) 1b2) to make long (tent cords) 1b3) to grow long, continue long Aramaic equivalent: a.rakh (אֲרִיךְ "be proper" H0749)

H749.def: v 1) (CLBL) (P'al) to be long, reach, meet adj v 2)(BDB/TWOT) fitting, proper Aramaic of a.rakh (אָרַךְ "to prolong" H0748)

H751.def: Erech = "long" a city 40 miles (64 km) northwest of Ur toward Babylon on the left bank of the Euphrates river Also named: ar.ke.vay (אַרְכְּוָיֵא "Erech" H0756)

H752.def: 1) long 1a) of time 1b) of God's wisdom (fig.)

H753.def: 1) length 1a) physical length 1b) of time 2) forbearance, self-restraint (of patience)

H754.def: prolongation, lengthening, prolonging Aramaic of e.rekh (עֵ֫רֶךְ "valuation" H6187)

H755.def: knee Aramaic of av.rekh (אַבְרֵךְ "to bow" H0086)

H756.def: Aramaic of e.rekh (אֶ֫רֶךְ "Erech" H0751) § Archevites = "lengthy" a native of the city of Erech

H757.def: Someone from Arach(?), first mentioned at Jos.16.2 § Archite or Archi = "lengthy" referring to the home city of Hushai

H758.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.10.22; son of: Shem (H8035); brother of: Asshur (H0804H), Elam (H5867C), Arpachshad (H0775) and Lud (H3865); father of: Uz (H5780), Hul (H2343), Gether (H1666) and Mash (H4851) § Aram or Arameans = "exalted" 1) Aram or Syrian nation 2) the Syrian or Aramean people Aram = "exalted" n m 3) fifth son of Shem 4) a grandson of Nahor 5) a descendant of Asher

H759.def: : palace citadel, palace, fortress Also means: ar.mon (אַרְמוֹן ": fortress" H0759H)

H760.def: § Aram-zobah = "exalted station: exalted conflict" probably the land northeast of Damascus

H761.def: A woman of Syria living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, only mentioned at 1Ch.7.14; married to Manasseh (H4519); father of: Machir (H4353) § Syrian or Aramean = "exalted" a thing or a person from Syria or Aram

H762.def: § Aramaic = "language" the language of Aram, Aramaic

H763.def: § Aram-naharaim = "Aram of the two rivers" Mesopotamia

H764.def: A man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 2Sa.21.8; son of: Saul (H7586G) and Rizpah (H7532); brother of: Mephibosheth (H4648H); half-brother of: Jonathan (H3083H), Abinadab (H0041H), Malchi-shua (H4444), Merab (H4764), Michal (H4324) and Ish-bosheth (H0378) § Armoni = "one of the palace" a son of Saul

H765.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.36.28; son of: Dishan (H1789); brother of: Uz (H5780I) § Aran = "joyous" a descendant of Esau

H767.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.2.25; son of: Jerahmeel (H3396); brother of: Ram (H7410H), Bunah (H0946), Ozem (H0684H), Ahijah (H0281J) and Onam (H0208H) § Oren = "cedar" a descendant of Judah

H768.def: 1) hare 1a) probably an extinct animal because no known hare chews its cud, exact meaning is unknown, and best left untranslated as "arnebeth"

H769.def: § Arnon = "rushing stream" a river and surrounding valley in south Palestine, forms the border between Moab and the Amorites

H770.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.3.21; son of: Hananiah (H2608L); brother of: Pelatiah (H6410), Jeshaiah (H3470B), Rephaiah (H7509), Obadiah (H5662H) and Shecaniah (H7935) § Arnan = "lion of perpetuity: or a wild goat: I shall shout for joy" a descendant of David

H771.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.24.16; also called Araunah at 2Sa.24.16,18,20,21,22,23,24; Also named: a.rav.nah (אֲרַוְנָה "Araunah" H0728) § Ornan = "light was perpetuated: their fir trees" a Jebusite who sold, to David, a threshing floor for an altar

H772.def: : soil earth, world, ground Aramaic of e.rets (אֶ֫רֶץ ": soil" H0776H)

H773.def: bottom Aramaic of e.rets (אֶ֫רֶץ ": soil" H0776H)

H774.def: § Arpad or Arphad = "I shall be spread out (or supported)" a city in northern Syria cited as an example of the Assyrian conquest

H775.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.10.22; son of: Shem (H8035); brother of: Asshur (H0804H), Elam (H5867C), Lud (H3865) and Aram (H0758); father of: Cainan (G2536); also called Arphaxad at Luk.3.36; Also named: Arphaxad (Ἀρφαξάδ "Arphaxad" G0742) § Arphaxad = "I shall fail as the breast: he cursed the breast-bottle" third son of Shem

H776.def: : country/field 1) land, earth 1a) earth 1a1) whole earth (as opposed to a part) 1a2) earth (as opposed to heaven) 1a3) earth (inhabitants) 1b) land 1b1) country, territory 1b2) district, region 1b3) tribal territory 1b4) piece of ground 1b5) land of Canaan, Israel 1b6) inhabitants of land 1b7) Sheol, land without return, (under) world 1b8) city (-state) 1c) ground, surface of the earth 1c1) ground 1c2) soil 1d) (in phrases) 1d1) people of the land 1d2) space or distance of country (in measurements of distance) 1d3) level or plain country 1d4) land of the living 1d5) end(s) of the earth 1e) (almost wholly late in usage) 1e1) lands, countries 1e1a) often in contrast to Canaan

H777.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.16.9 § Arza = "earthy" housekeeper of Elah, king of Israel

H778.def: earth, the earth Aramaic of e.rets (אֶ֫רֶץ ": soil" H0776H)

H779.def: 1) to curse 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to curse 1a2) cursed be he (participle used as in curses) 1b) (Niphal) to be cursed, cursed 1c)(Piel) to curse, lay under a curse, put a curse on 1d) (Hophal) to be made a curse, be cursed

H780.def: § Ararat = "the curse reversed: precipitation of curse" a mountainous region of eastern Armenia, between the river Araxes and the lakes Van and Oroomiah, the site where Noah's ark came to rest

H781.def: 1) to betroth, engage 1a) (Piel) to betroth (man or woman) 1b) (Pual) to be betrothed

H784.def: 1) fire 1a) fire, flames 1b) supernatural fire (accompanying theophany) 1c) fire (for cooking, roasting, parching) 1d) altar-fire 1e) God's anger (fig.) Aramaic equivalent: esh (אֶשָּׁא "fire" H0785)

H788.def: A man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Gen.46.21; son of: Benjamin (H1144); brother of: Bela (H1106A), Becher (H1071), Gera (H1617), Naaman (H5283), Ehi (H0278), Rosh (H7220), Muppim (H4649), Huppim (H2650H), Ard (H0714), Ahiram (H0297) and Jediael (H3043) Also named: ash.be.li (אַשְׁבֵּלִי "Ashbelite" H0789) § Ashbel = "a man in God: a man of Baal: fire of Bel: I will make a path" second son of Benjamin

H789.def: Someone descended from Ashbel, a man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Gen.46.21; son of: Benjamin (H1144); brother of: Bela (H1106A), Becher (H1071), Gera (H1617), Naaman (H5283), Ehi (H0278), Rosh (H7220), Muppim (H4649), Huppim (H2650H), Ard (H0714), Ahiram (H0297) and Jediael (H3043) Group of ash.bel (אַשְׁבֵּל "Ashbel" H0788) § Ashbelites = "a man in God: a man of Baal: fire of Bel: I will make a path" descendants of Ashbel

H790.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.36.26; son of: Dishon (H1787); brother of: Hemdan (H2533), Ithran (H3506) and Cheran (H3763) § Eshban = "fire of discernment" an chief of Edom

H791.def: Ashbea = "I shall make to swear" the patriarch of a family of Judah Combined with ba.yit (בַּ֫יִת "Beth" H1004)

H792.def: A man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.2.8; son of: Saul (H7586G); brother of: Jonathan (H3083H), Abinadab (H0041H), Malchi-shua (H4444), Merab (H4764), Michal (H4324), Armoni (H0764), Mephibosheth (H4648H) Another name of ish-bo.shet (אִישׁ־בֹ֫שֶׁת "Ish-bosheth" H0378) § Eshbaal = "a man of Baal" the fourth son of Saul, also called Ishbosheth

H795.def: § Ashdod = "powerful" a major Philistine city on the Mediterranean Sea west from Jerusalem, modern Esdud

H796.def: Ashdodite = "I will spoil" an inhabitant of Ashdod Another spelling of ash.dod (אַשְׁדּוֹד "Ashdod" H0795)

H797.def: Ashdodites = "I will spoil" language of Ashdod Another spelling of ash.dod (אַשְׁדּוֹד "Ashdod" H0795)

H798.def: Ashdoth-pisgah = "slopes of Pisgah" the mountains of Pisgah, which include Mt. Nebo Combined with pis.gah (פִּסְגָּה "Pisgah" H6449)

H799.def: fiery law, fire of a law, fire was a law (meaning uncertain)

H801.def: food offering, offering given to God by fire or eaten by priests, sacrificial offering

H802.def: : woman 1) woman, wife, female 1a) woman (opposite of man) 1b) wife (woman married to a man) 1c) female (of animals) 1d) each, every (pronoun)

H803.def: buttress, support Aramaic equivalent: osh (אֹשׁ "foundation" H0787)

H804.def: § Asshur or Assyria = "a step" the nation, Assyria the land, Assyria or Asshur

H806.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at 1Ch.2.24; son of: Hezron (H2696H) and Abiah (H0029J); half-brother of: Ram (H7410), Jerahmeel (H3396), Caleb (H3612H) and Segub (H7687H); married to Helah (H2458) and Naarah (H5292B); father of: Ahuzzam (H0275), Hepher (H2660), Temeni (H8488), Haahashtari (H0326), Zereth (H6889), Izhar (H3328), Ethnan (H0869) and Koz (H6976) § Ashur = "black" son of Pharez

H807.def: § Ashima = "guiltiness: I will make desolate" a god of Hamath

H808.def: with sense of pressing down, foundation

H809.def: 1) raisin-cake, used in sacrificial feasts 1a) consistently translated in AV as "flagon of wine"

H811.def: 1) cluster 1a) of grapes 1b) of flowers (metaphor of lover)

H812.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.14.13; brother of: Mamre (H4471H) and Aner (H6063) § Eshcol = "cluster" 1) an area of Hebron, the valley of Eshcol 2) an Amorite, the brother of Mamre, dwelling in Hebron

H813.def: A man of the Gomerians living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.10.3; son of: Gomer (H1586); brother of: Riphath (H7384A) and Togarmah (H8425) § Ashchenaz or Ashkenaz = "a man as sprinkled: fire as scattered" 1) a descendant of Japheth 2) a northern people, perhaps of Bithynia

H816.def: 1) to offend, be guilty, trespass 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to do wrong, offend, trespass, commit an offense, do injury 1a2) to be or become guilty 1a3) to be held guilty 1a4) to be incriminated 1b) (Niphal) to suffer punishment 1c) (Hiphil) to declare guilty 2) (TWOT) to be desolate, acknowledge offense

H817.def: 1) guilt, offense, guiltiness 1a) offense, trespass, fault 1b) guilt, guiltiness 1c) compensation (for offense) 1d) trespass offering, guilt offering

H818.def: guilty, faulty (and obliged to offer a guilt-offering)

H819.def: 1) guiltiness, guilt, offense, sin, wrong-doing 1a) doing wrong, committing a trespass or offense 1b) becoming guilty, guilt 1c) bringing a guilt-offering

H820.def: 1) the stout, among the stout 2) (CLBL) the desolate, like dead

H823.def: § Ashnah = "I will cause change" a town in Judah

H825.def: 1) necromancer, conjurer, 2) (TWOT) astrologer, enchanter, exorcist Aramaic equivalent: ash.shaph (אָשַׁף "enchanter" H0826)

H826.def: conjurer, enchanter, (CLBL) necromancer Aramaic of ash.shaph (אַשָּׁף "enchanter" H0825)

H827.def: 1) quiver (for arrows) 1a) of home, of God's instruments (fig.)

H828.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Dan.1.3 § Ashpenaz = "I will make prominent the sprinkled" chief eunuch of Nebuchadnezzar

H829.def: piece of meat, measured portion, date-cake (meaning and significance obscure)

H831.def: Askelon or Ashkelon = "the fire of infamy: I shall be weighed" a maritime city of the Philistines, southwest of Jerusalem Also named: esh.qe.lo.ni (אֶשְׁקְלוֹנִי "Ashkelon" H0832)

H832.def: Eshkalonites = "the fire of infamy: I shall be weighed" an inhabitant of Ashkelon Another spelling of ash.qe.lon (אַשְׁקְלוֹן "Ashkelon" H0831)

H833.def: 1) to go straight, walk, go on, advance, make progress 1a)(Qal) to go straight on, make progress 1b) (Piel) 1b1) to go straight on, advance 1b2) to lead on (causative) 1b3) to set right, righten 1b4) to pronounce happy, call blessed 1c) (Pual) 1c1) to be advanced, be led on 1c2) to be made happy, be blessed

H835.def: 1) happiness, blessedness 1a) often used as interjection 1b) blessed are

H836.def: A man of the tribe of Asher living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.30.13; son of: Israel (H3478) and Zilpah (H2153); brother of: Gad (H1410); half-brother of: Reuben (H7205), Simeon (H8095), Levi (H3878), Judah (H3063), Dan (H1835H), Naphtali (H5321), Issachar (H3485), Zebulun (H2074), Dinah (H1783), Joseph (H3130) and Benjamin (H1144); father of: Imnah (H3232), Ishvah (H3438), Ishvi (H3440), Beriah (H1283) and Serah (H8294); also translated "pine" or "cypress wood" at Ezk.27.6(?); Ashurite at 2Sa.2.9(?); § Asher = "happy" 1) son of Jacob and Zilpah 2) the tribe descended from Asher 3) a city east of Shechem in Manasseh

H840.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.4.16; son of: Jehallelel (H3094); brother of: Ziph (H2128J), Ziphah (H2129) and Tiria (H8493) § Asareel = "God holds" a descendant of Judah through Caleb

H841.def: A man of the tribe of Levi living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 1Ch.25.2; son of: Asaph (H0623H); brother of: Zaccur (H2139J), Joseph (H3130I) and Nethaniah (H5418H); also called Jesharelah at 1Ch.25.14; Also named: ye.sar.e.lah (יְשַׂרְאֵ֫לָה "Jesharelah" H3480) § Asarelah = "God holds" a son of Asaph, appointed sanctuary musician by David

H842.def: § Ashera(h) = "groves (for idol worship)" a Babylonian (Astarte)-Canaanite goddess (of fortune and happiness), the supposed consort of Baal, her images her images sacred trees or poles set up near an altar

H843.def: Someone from the tribe of Asher who was living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.30.13; son of: Israel (H3478) and Zilpah (H2153); brother of: Gad (H1410); half-brother of: Reuben (H7205), Simeon (H8095), Levi (H3878), Judah (H3063), Dan (H1835H), Naphtali (H5321), Issachar (H3485), Zebulun (H2074), Dinah (H1783), Joseph (H3130) and Benjamin (H1144); father of: Imnah (H3232), Ishvah (H3438), Ishvi (H3440), Beriah (H1283) and Serah (H8294); also translated "pine" or "cypress wood" at Ezk.27.6(?); Ashurite at 2Sa.2.9(?); Group of a.sher (אָשֵׁר "Asher" H0836) § Asherites = "happy" those descended from Asher

H844.def: A man of the tribe of Manasseh living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Num.26.31; son of: Gilead (H1568H); brother of: Abiezer (H0044I), Helek (H2507), Shechem (H7928), Shemida (H8061) and Hepher (H2660A) Also named: as.ri.e.li (אַשְׂרִיאֵלִי "Asrielite" H0845) § Ashriel or Asriel = "I shall be prince of God" 1) a great-grandson of Manasseh, and son of Gilead 2) a son of Manasseh

H845.def: Someone descended from Asriel who was a man of the tribe of Manasseh living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Num.26.31; son of: Gilead (H1568H); brother of: Abiezer (H0044I), Helek (H2507), Shechem (H7928), Shemida (H8061) and Hepher (H2660A) Group of as.ri.el (אַשְׂרִיאֵל "Asriel" H0844) § Asrielites = "I shall be prince of God" the clan descended from Ashriel

H846.def: wall, panelling, beams, structure (precise meaning and derivation uncertain) Aramaic of shur (שׁוּר "wall" H7791A)

H847.def: Eshtaol = "entreaty" a Danite city located within Judah Also named: esh.ta.u.li (אֶשְׁתָּאֻלִי "Eshtaolite" H0848)

H848.def: Group of esh.ta.ol (אֶשְׁתָּאוֹל "Eshtaol" H0847) § Eshtaulites = "I will be entreated" the inhabitants of Eshtaol

H850.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 1Ch.4.11; son of: Mehir (H4243); father of: Beth-rapha (H1051), Paseah (H6454), Tehinnah (H8468) § Eshton = "effeminate" a descendant of Caleb of tribe of Judah

H851.def: § Eshtemoa = "I will make myself heard" Eshtemoh = "I shall cause my own ruin: fire of astonishment: I shall soar aloft" a Levitical city in Judah

H852.def: sign, miraculous signs, wonders Aramaic of ot (אוֹת ": miraculous" H0226)

H853.def: sign of the definite direct object, not translated in English but generally preceding and indicating the accusative Aramaic equivalent: yat (יָת "whom" H3487)

H854.def: 1) with, near, together with 1a) with, together with 1b) with (of relationship) 1c) near (of place) 1d) with (poss.) 1e) from...with, from (with other prep)

H856.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.16.31; father of: Jezebel (H0348) § Ethbaal = "with Baal" king of Sidon, father of Jezebel

H857.def: 1) to come, arrive 1a) (Qal) to come (of men, time, beasts, calamity) 1b) (Hiphil) to bring Aramaic equivalent: a.tah (אֲתָה "to come" H0858)

H858.def: 1) to come, arrive 1a) (P'al) to come 1b) (Aphel) to bring 1c) (Hophal) to be brought 2) used in the NT in the phrase "maranatha" -"Lord come" Aramaic of a.tah (אָתָה "to come" H0857)

H863.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.15.19 § Ittai or Ithai = "with me" 1) a Gittite commander from Gath in David's army 2) one of David's 30 mighty warriors, a Benjamite

H864.def: § Etham = "with them: their plowshare" a stopping place for Israel during the Exodus

H865.def: 1) yesterday, recently, formerly 1a) yesterday 1b) formerly (fig.) 1c) from yesterday, already (in phrase) 2) (TWOT +) before, before that time, before the time, heretofore, of late, of old, these days, time(s) past

H867.def: A man of the tribe of Levi living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 1Ch.6.21; son of: Zerah (H2226J); father of: Malchijah (H4441); also called Ethni at 1Ch.6.41; Another name of ye.a.te.ray (יְאָֽתְרַי "Jeatherai" H2979) § Ethni = "my hire" an ancestor of Asaph

H868.def: 1) hire of prostitute, price 1a) hire (of harlot) 1b) of idolatrous Israel, Jerusalem, Tyre (fig.)

H869.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.4.7; son of: Ashhur (H0806) and Helah (H2458); brother of: Haahashtari (H0326), Zereth (H6889), Izhar (H3328) and Koz (H6976); half-brother of: Ahuzzam (H0275), Hepher (H2660) and Temeni (H8488) § Ethnan = "hire of unchastity" a grandson of Ashur

H871.def: This name means perhaps mountain pass or caravan route

H872.def: 1) entrance, a coming in, entering, entry (of the temple) 1a) entrance 1b) entering (act of entrance)

H873.def: evil, bad, be evil Aramaic of ba.ash (בָּאַשׁ "to stink" H0887)

H874.def: 1) (Piel) 1a) to make plain, distinct 1b) to make clear 1c) to declare 1d) letters on a tablet

H876.def: § Beer = "well" an oasis rest in desert during the Exodus

H878.def: A man of the tribe of Asher living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.7.37; son of: Zophah (H6690); brother of: Suah (H5477), Harnepher (H2774), Shual (H7777B), Beri (H1275), Imrah (H3236), Bezer (H1221H), Hod (H1936), Shamma (H8037H), Shilshah (H8030) and Ithran (H3506H) § Beera = "a well" a man of Asher

H879.def: § Beer-elim = "well of God" a well in the south of Moab

H880.def: A man of the tribe of Reuben living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.5.6; son of: Baal (H1168) § Beerah = "well" a Reubenite chief exiled to Assyria

H881.def: § Beeroth = "wells" a Gibeonite city allotted to Benjamin

H882.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, only mentioned at Gen.26.34; married to Anah (H6034); father of: Judith (H3067) § Beeri = "my well" 1) Esau's Hittite father-in-law 2) father of Hosea

H883.def: § Beer-lahai-roi = "well of the Living One seeing me" a well west of Kadesh, south of Israel

H884.def: § Beer-sheba = "well of the sevenfold oath" a city at the south edge of Israel

H1083.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.24.14 § Bilgah = "cheerfulness" 1) a priest of the 15th course, in David's time 2) a priest accompanying Zerubbabel

H1084.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Neh.10.8; also called Bilgai at Neh.10.8; Another name of bil.gah (בִּלְגָּה "Bilgah" H1083H) § Bilgai = "my cheerfulness" a priest who covenanted with Nehemiah

H1085.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Job.2.11 § Bildad = "confusing (by mingling) love" the second friend of Job

H1086.def: 1) to wear out, become old 1a) (Qal) to wear out 1b) (Piel) 1b1) to wear out 1b2) to wear out by use, use up completely 1b3) to enjoy, use to the full Aramaic equivalent: be.la (בְּלָא "to wear out" H1080)

H1088.def: § Balah = "waxed old" a place in Simeon

H1092.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.36.27; son of: Ezer (H0687); brother of: Zaavan (H2190) and Akan (H3292) § Bilhan = "their decrepitude" 1) a descendant of Esau 2) a Benjamite

H1095.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezk.14.14; also called Belteshazzar at Dan.1.7; 2.26; 4.8,9,18,19; 5.12; 10.1; Another name of da.niy.yel (דָּנִיֵּאל, דָּנִאֵל "Daniel" H1840G) § Belteshazzar = "lord of the straitened's treasure" 1) the 4th of the greater prophets, taken as hostage in the first deportation to Babylon; because of the gift of God of the interpretation of dreams, he became the 2nd in command of the Babylon empire and lasted through the end of the Babylonian empire and into the Persian empire. His prophecies are the key to the understanding of end time events. Noted for his purity and holiness by contemporary prophet, Ezekiel 1a) also, 'Daniel' (H1840 or H1841)

H1096.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezk.14.14; also called Belteshazzar at Dan.1.7; 2.26; 4.8,9,18,19; 5.12; 10.1; Aramaic of da.niy.yel (דָּנִיֵּאל, דָּנִאֵל "Daniel" H1840) § Belteshazzar = "lord of the straitened's treasure" 1) the 4th of the greater prophets, taken as hostage in the first deportation to Babylon; because of the gift of God of the interpretation of dreams, he became the 2nd in command of the Babylon empire and lasted through the end of the Babylonian empire and into the Persian empire. His prophecies are the key to the understanding of end time events. Noted for his purity and holiness by contemporary prophet, Ezekiel 1a) also, 'Daniel' (H1840 or H1841)

H1097.def: subst 1) wearing out adv of negation 2) without, no, not

H1100.def: A name like sa.tan (שָׂטָן "Satan" H7854) This name means worthless, base, wicked

H1103.def: (Qal) to gather figs, tend sycamore trees

H1104.def: 1) to swallow down, swallow up, engulf, eat up 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to swallow down 1a2) to swallow up, engulf 1b) (Niphal) to be swallowed up 1c) (Piel) 1c1) to swallow 1c2) to swallow up, engulf 1c3) squandering (fig.) 1d) (Pual) to be swallowed up 1e) (Hithpael) to be ended

H1105.def: 1) a swallowing, devouring (fig. for ruin) 2) a thing swallowed

H1108.def: Someone descended from Bela who was a man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Gen.46.21; son of: Benjamin (H1144); brother of: Becher (H1071), Ashbel (H0788), Gera (H1617), Naaman (H5283), Ehi (H0278), Rosh (H7220), Muppim (H4649), Huppim (H2650H), Ard (H0714), Ahiram (H0297) and Jediael (H3043); father of: Ard (H0714H), Naaman (H5283H), Ezbon (H0675H), Uzzi (H5813I), Uzziel (H5816I), Jerimoth (H3406), Iri (H5901), Addar (H0146H), Gera (H1617I), Abihud (H0031), Abishua (H0050H) and Ahoah (H0265) Group of be.la (בֶּ֫לַע "Bela" H1106A) § Belaites = "swallowing" descendants of Bela

H1110.def: 1) to waste, lay waste, devastate 1a) (Poel) to make waste 1b) (Pual) devastated (participle)

H1111.def: A man of the Moabites living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Num.22.2; son of: Zippor (H6834) Also named: Balak (Βαλάκ "Balak" G0904) § Balak = "devastator" a king of Moab who hired Balaam to curse Israel

H1112.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Dan.5.1; son of: Nebuchadnezzar (H5019) Also named: be.le.shats.tsar (בֵּֽלְאשַׁצַּר, בֵּלְשַׁאצַּר "Belshazzar" H1113) § Belshazzar = "Bel protect the king" king of Babylon at the time of its fall; he to whom Daniel interpreted the writing on the wall

H1113.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Dan.5.1; son of: Nebuchadnezzar (H5019) Aramaic of be.le.shats.tsar (בֵּֽלְאשַׁצַּר, בֵּלְשַׁאצַּר "Belshazzar" H1112) § Belshazzar = "Bel protect the king" king of Babylon at the time of its fall; he to whom Daniel interpreted the writing on the wall

H1114.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezr.2.2 § Bilshan = "in slander" an exile who returned with Zerubbabel

H1115.def: subst 1) not, except adv 2) not 3) except (after preceding negation) conj 4) except (after an implied or expressed negation) with prep 5) so as not, in order not 6) an account of not, because...not 7) until not

H1116.def: 1) high place, ridge, height, bamah (technical name for cultic platform) 1a) high place, mountain 1b) high places, battlefields 1c) high places (as places of worship) 1d) funeral mound?

H1117.def: Bamah = "high place" a place in Palestine (of places of idolatrous worship) Another name of giv.on (גִּבְעוֹן "Gibeon" H1391)

H1118.def: A man of the tribe of Asher living before Israel's Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.7.33; son of: Japhlet (H3310); brother of: Pasach (H6457) and Ashvath (H6220) § Bimhal = "in circumcision: in weakness (by mixture)" a descendant of Asher

H1120.def: § Bamoth = "high places" or "great high place" a town on the river Arnon in Moab

H1122.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.15.18 § Ben = "son" a Levite, one of the porters appointed by David for the ark

H1123.def: son, child Aramaic of ben (בֵּן ": child" H1121)

H1124.def: 1) to build 1a) (P'al) to build 1b) (Ithp'il) to be built

H1125.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.4.11; son of: Abinadab (H0041J); married to Taphath (H2955) § Ben-Abinadab = "son of Abinadab" an officer of Solomon

H1126.def: A man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.35.18; son of: Israel (H3478) and Rachel (H7354); brother of: Joseph (H3130); half-brother of: Reuben (H7205), Simeon (H8095), Levi (H3878), Judah (H3063), Dan (H1835H), Naphtali (H5321), Gad (H1410), Asher (H0836), Issachar (H3485), Zebulun (H2074) and Dinah (H1783); father of: Bela (H1106A), Becher (H1071), Ashbel (H0788), Gera (H1617), Naaman (H5283), Ehi (H0278), Rosh (H7220), Muppim (H4649), Huppim (H2650H), Ard (H0714), Ahiram (H0297) and Jediael (H3043); also called Jamin at 1Sa.9.1,4; 22.7; 1x Another name of bin.ya.min (בִּנְיָמִין "Benjamin" H1144G) § Ben-oni = "son of my sorrow" the name given to Benjamin by Rachel

H1127.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.4.13; son Geber (H1398) § Ben-geber = "the son of Geber" or "the son of a man" an officer of Solomon

H1128.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.4.9; son of: Deker (H1857) § Ben-dekar = "son of stabbing" or "son of Dekar" an officer of Solomon

H1129.def: 1) to build, rebuild, establish, cause to continue 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to build, rebuild 1a2) to build a house (ie, establish a family) 1b) (Niphal) 1b1) to be built 1b2) to be rebuilt 1b3) established (of restored exiles) (fig.) 1b4) established (made permanent) 1b5) to be built up (of childless wife becoming the mother of a family through the children of a concubine) Aramaic equivalent: be.nah (בְּנָה "to build" H1124)

H1130.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 1Ki.15.18; son of: Tabrimmon (H2886) § Ben-hadad = "son of [the false god] Hadad" 1) the king of Syria, contemporary with Asa of Judah 2) the son of Hazael, also king of Syria

H1131.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezr.2.10; also called Binnui at Neh.7.15; Another spelling of ba.ni (בָּנִי "Bani" H1137Q) § Binnui = "built up" 1) an exile returnee with Zerubbabel, son of Henadad, who assisted at the reparation of the wall of Jerusalem, under Nehemiah 2) a Levite in time of Ezra, father of Noadiah 3) another Levite in time of Ezra, who took a foreign wife 4) still another Levite in time of Ezra, who took a foreign wife

H1132.def: A man of the tribe of Judah(?) living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.4.20; son of: Ishi (H3469H); brother of: Zoheth (H2105) § Ben-zoheth = "son of Zoheth" one of the tribe of Judah

H1133.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.4.8; son of: Hur (H1133H) § Ben-hur = "son of whiteness" or "son of Chur" a man who was commissariat officer for Solomon in Mount Ephraim

H1134.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 2Ch.17.7 § Ben-hail = "son of strength" or "son (man) of might" a ruler (prince) under Jehoshaphat

H1135.def: A man of the tribe of Judah(?) living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.4.20; son of: Shimon (H7889); brother of: Amnon (H0550H), Rinnah (H7441) and Tilon (H8436) § Ben-hanan = "son of favour" one of the tribe of Judah

H1136.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.4.10; son of: Hesed (H2618) § Ben-hesed = "son of mercy" an officer of Solomon

H1137.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 2Sa.23.36 § Bani = "built" 1) a Gadite, one of David's mighty warriors 2) a Levite of the line of Merari, and forefather to Ethan 3) a man of Judah of the line of Pharez 4) "children of Bani" returned from captivity with Zerubbabel 5) one or up to three Levites in Nehemiah's time

H1138.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Neh.9.4 § Bunni = "built" 1) a Levite in the time of Nehemiah 2) another Levite of earlier date

H1139.def: § Bene-barak = "sons of lightning" a city in Dan

H1141.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.8.18; son of: Jehoiada (H3077); father of: Ammizabad (H5990) § Benaiah = "Jehovah has built" or "Yahweh has built up" 1) one of David's mighty warriors, son of Jehoiada the chief priest, a Levite, set by David over his bodyguard, later having remained faithful to Solomon during Adonijah's attempt on the crown, was raised into the place of Joab as commander-in-chief of the army 2) the Pirathonite, an Ephraimite, one of David's thirty mighty warriors, and the captain of the eleventh monthly course 3) a Simeonite, a prince of the families of Simeon 4) a Levite in the time of David who played with a psaltery on Alamoth 5) a priest in the time of David, appointed to blow the trumpet before the ark 6) a Levite of the sons of Asaph 7) a Levite in the time of Hezekiah 8) Four Israelites in the time of Ezra who had taken strange wives 9) the father of Pelatiah

H1142.def: § Bene-jaakan = "sons of twisting" an Israelite place of encampment in the wilderness (same as be.e.rot be.ne-ya.a.qan (בְּאֵרֹת בְּנֵי־יַעֲקָן "Beeroth-bene-jaakan" H0885))

H1143.def: 1) between, space between two armies 1a) man of the space between armies, i.e. champion (of Goliath) (meton)

H1144.def: A man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.35.18; son of: Israel (H3478) and Rachel (H7354); brother of: Joseph (H3130); half-brother of: Reuben (H7205), Simeon (H8095), Levi (H3878), Judah (H3063), Dan (H1835H), Naphtali (H5321), Gad (H1410), Asher (H0836), Issachar (H3485), Zebulun (H2074) and Dinah (H1783); father of: Bela (H1106A), Becher (H1071), Ashbel (H0788), Gera (H1617), Naaman (H5283), Ehi (H0278), Rosh (H7220), Muppim (H4649), Huppim (H2650H), Ard (H0714), Ahiram (H0297) and Jediael (H3043); also called Jamin at 1Sa.9.1,4; 22.7; 1x § Benjamin = "son of the right hand" 1) Jacob's and Rachel's youngest son, Joseph's full brother 2) son of Bilhan, great-grandson of Benjamin 3) a Benjamite, one of the sons of Harim, in the time of Ezra who had taken a strange wife 4) the tribe descended from Benjamin, the son of Jacob

H1145.def: Someone from the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.35.18; son of: Israel (H3478) and Rachel (H7354); brother of: Joseph (H3130); half-brother of: Reuben (H7205), Simeon (H8095), Levi (H3878), Judah (H3063), Dan (H1835H), Naphtali (H5321), Gad (H1410), Asher (H0836), Issachar (H3485), Zebulun (H2074) and Dinah (H1783); father of: Bela (H1106A), Becher (H1071), Ashbel (H0788), Gera (H1617), Naaman (H5283), Ehi (H0278), Rosh (H7220), Muppim (H4649), Huppim (H2650H), Ard (H0714), Ahiram (H0297) and Jediael (H3043); also called Jamin at 1Sa.9.1,4; 22.7; 1x Group of bin.ya.min (בִּנְיָמִין "Benjamin" H1144) § Benjamite = "a son of the right hand" one of the tribe of Benjamin

H1146.def: structure, building Aramaic equivalent: bin.yan (בִּנְיָן "building" H1147)

H1147.def: a building, structure Aramaic of bin.yan (בִּנְיָן "building" H1146)

H1148.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Neh.10.13 § Beninu = "our son" a returning Levite exile who sealed the covenant with Nehemiah

H1150.def: A man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 1Ch.8.37; son of: Moza (H4162H); father of: Raphah (H7498), Eleasah (H0501H) and Azel (H0682A) § Binea or Bineah = fountain a descendant of Jonathan

H1151.def: A man of Ammon living at the time of the Patriarchs, only mentioned at Gen.19.38; son of: Lot (H3876) and his second daughter (H3876I); half-brother of Moab (H4124H) § Ben-ami = "son of my people" son of Lot, born to his second daughter, progenitor of the Ammonites

H1152.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Neh.3.6; father of: Meshullam (H4918S) § Besodeiah = "with the counsel of Jehovah" or "in the secret of the Lord" father of one of the repairers of the wall of Jerusalem in the time of Nehemiah

H1153.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezr.2.49 § Besai = "my treading" a temple slave (or group of slaves) who returned with Zerubbabel

H1154.def: unripe or sour grapes Another spelling of bo.ser (בֹּ֫סֶר "unripe grape" H1155)

H1155.def: unripe grapes, sour grapes Also means: be.ser (בֶּסֶר "unripe grape" H1154)

H1156.def: 1) to ask, seek, request, desire, pray, make petition 1a) (P'al) 1a1) to ask, request 1a2) to seek (for favour)

H1157.def: 1) behind, through, round about, on behalf of, away from, about 1a) through (of action) 1b) behind (with verbs of shutting) 1c) about (with verbs of fencing) 1d) on behalf of (metaph. especially with Hithpael)

H1158.def: 1) to seek out, swell, cause to swell, boil up, enquire 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to seek, enquire 1a2) to cause to boil up 1b) (Niphal) 1b1) to be searched out 1b2) to be swelling, bulging, swelling out

H1159.def: 1) petition, request 2) prayer (always in Jewish liturgy)

H1160.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.36.32; father of: Bela (H1106) § Beor = "burning" 1) father of Balaam 2) father of Bela, king of Edom

H1162.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living before Israel's Monarchy, first mentioned at Rut.2.1; son of: Salmon (H8007) and Rahab (H7343); married to Ruth (H7327); father of: Obed (H5744) Also named: Booz (Βοόζ "Boaz" G1003) § Boaz = "fleetness" 1) ancestor of David, kinsman-redeemer to Ruth, daughter-in-law of Naomi 2) name of the left of two brazen pillars, 18 cubits high, erected in the porch of Solomon's temple

H1163.def: 1) to kick, kick at 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to kick 1a2) to kick at 1a3) to desire (fig.)

H1164.def: ruin, heap of ruins Another spelling of i (עִי "ruin" H5856)

H1166.def: This name means to be married Another name of ye.ru.sha.laim (יְרוּשָׁלִַ֫ם, יְרוּשְׁלֵם "Jerusalem" H3389)

H1167.def: : master/leader 1) owner, husband, lord 1a) owner 1b) a husband 1c) citizens, inhabitants 1d) rulers, lords 1e) (noun of relationship used to characterise-ie, master of dreams)

H1169.def: owner, lord Aramaic of ba.al (בַּעַל ": master" H1167)

H1170.def: § Baal-berith = "lord of the covenant" a god of the Philistines

H1171.def: Baal-gad = "lord of fortune" a city noted for Baal-worship, located at the most northern or northwestern point to which Joshua's victories extended Another name of cher.mon (חֶרְמוֹן "(Mount )Hermon" H2768)

H1172.def: 1) mistress, female owner 2) sorceress, necromancer (noun of relationship)

H1174.def: Baal-hamon = "lord (possessor) of abundance" the site of Solomon's vineyard Another spelling of cher.mon (חֶרְמוֹן "(Mount )Hermon" H2768)

H1175.def: Baalath or Bealoth = "mistresses" a town in south Judah Another name of ba.a.lat be.er (בַּעֲלַת בְּאֵר "Baalath-beer" H1192)

H1176.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Ki.1.2 § Baal-zebub = "lord of the fly" a Philistine deity worshipped at Ekron

H1177.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Gen.36.38; son of: Achbor (H5907) § Baal-hanan = "Baal is gracious" 1) an early king of Edom 2) one of David's officers, a Gederite, who had the superintendence of his olive and sycamore plantations

H1178.def: § Baal-hazor = "lord of the village" a town on the border between Ephraim and Benjamin, apparent location of a sheep farm of Absalom and location of Amnon's murder

H1179.def: Baal-hermon or Baal-chermon = "lord of destruction" a city near or on Mount Hermon, named as a seat of Baal worship Another name of cher.mon (חֶרְמוֹן "(Mount )Hermon" H2768)

H1180.def: Baali = "my lord" a deity in the northern kingdom, variation of the name 'Baal' Another name of ye.ho.vah (יהוה "LORD" H3068G)

H1181.def: Baale-bamoth = "the lords of the high places" the people of Arnon, east of Jordan

H1182.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.5.16; son of: David (H1732); brother of: Amnon (H0550), Chileab (H3609), Absalom (H0053), Adonijah (H0138), Shephatiah (H8203), Ithream (H3507), Shammua (H8051H), Shobab (H7727), Nathan (H5416), Solomon (H8010), Ibhar (H2984), Elishua (H0474), Nepheg (H5298H), Japhia (H3309I), Elishama (H0476H), Eliphelet (H0467), Tamar (H8559H), Elpelet (H0467I), Nogah (H5052) and Jerimoth (H3406N); also called Beeliada at 1Ch.14.7; Another name of el.ya.da (אֶלְיָדָע "Eliada" H0450G) § Beeliada = "the lord knows" a son of David, born in Jerusalem, elsewhere named 'Eliada'

H1183.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.12.5 § Bealiah = "Jehovah is master" a Benjamite who went over to David at Ziklag and was one of David's mighty warriors

H1184.def: Baale-Judah = "the lords of Judah" a place in Judah named for the Baalim, also known as Kirjath-jearim, Kirjath-baal; modern Kuriet el Enab Combined with qir.yat ye.a.rim (קִרְיַת יְעָרִים "Kiriath-jearim" H7157)

H1185.def: A man of the Ammonites living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at Jer.40.14 § Baalis = "lord of the banner: in causing the joy" king of the Ammonites at the time of the destruction of Jerusalem by Nebuchadnezzar

H1186.def: § Baal-meon = "lord of the habitation" a town in Reuben, mentioned in connection with Nebo, and in the time of Ezekiel, Moabite

H1187.def: Baal-peor = "lord of the gap" the deity worshipped at Peor with probable licentious rites Another name of ba.al (בַּעַל "Baal" H1168A)

H1188.def: § Baal-perazim = "lord of the breaks" the site of a victory of David over the Philistines, and of a great destruction of their images; also called 'Mount Perazim'

H1189.def: § Baal Tsphon or Baal-zephon = "lord of the north" a place in Egypt near the Red Sea where Pharaoh and his army were destroyed during the Exodus

H1190.def: § Baal-shalisha = "thrice-great lord" a place in Ephraim near Gilgal

H1192.def: § Baalath-beer = "mistress of the well" a town in the south part of Judah, given to Simeon, which also bore the name of 'Ramath-negeb'

H1193.def: § Baal-tamar = "lord of the palms" a place near Gibeah in Benjamin

H1194.def: Beon = "in the dwelling: indwelling" a place or city in Reuben Another name of ba.al me.on (בַּ֫עַל מְעוֹן "Baal-meon" H1186)

H1195.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.4.12; son of: Ahilud (H0286); brother of: Jehoshaphat (H3092) § Baana or Baanah = "in the affliction" 1) the son of Ahilud, Solomon's commissariat officer in Jezreel and the north of the Jordan valley 2) another of Solomon's officers 3) father of Zadok who assisted in rebuilding the wall of Jerusalem under Nehemiah

H1196.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.4.2; son of: Rimmon (H7417B); brother of: Rechab (H7394) § Baana or Baanah = "in affliction" 1) a Benjamite, son Rimmon, who with his brother Rechab murdered Ish-bosheth. For this, killed by David, mutilated bodies hung up over the pool at Hebron 2) a Netophathite, father of Heleb or Heled, one of David's mighty warriors 3) the head of a family of exiles returning with Zerubbabel 4) a chief of the people

H1199.def: A womanof the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.8.8; married to Shaharaim (H7842) § Baara = "brutish" one of the wives of Shaharaim, a Benjamite

H1201.def: A king of the Kingdom of Israel, living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 1Ki.15.16; son of: Ahijah (H0281I); father of: Elah (H0425H) § Baasha = "wicked" third king of the northern kingdom of Israel and the founder of its second dynasty having killed the second king, Nadab

H1202.def: A man of the tribe of Levi living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.6.40; son of: Malchijah (H4441); father of: Michael (H4317J) § Baaseiah = "in the service of Jehovah" a Gershonite Levite, one of the forefathers of Asaph the singer

H1203.def: Beeshterah = "with increase" a Levitical city in Manasseh, east of the Jordan; probably identical with 'Ashtaroth' Another name of ash.ta.rot (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת "Ashtaroth" H6252H)

H1204.def: 1) to terrify, startle, fall upon, dismay, be overtaken by sudden terror 1a) (Niphal) to be terrified 1b) (Piel) 1b1) to fall upon, overwhelm 1b2) to terrify

H1209.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezr.2.17 § Bezai = "conqueror" a chief of the returning exiles in the time of Ezra

H1212.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Exo.31.2; son of: Uri (H0221) § Bezaleel = "in the shadow (i.e. protection) of God" 1) son of Uri and grandson of Hur; a skilled Judahite artisan in all works of metal, wood, and stone and one of the architects of the tabernacle 2) an Israelite, one of the sons of Pahath-moab, in the time of Ezra who had taken a strange wife

H1213.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezr.2.52; also called Bazlith at Neh.7.54; § Bazlith or Bazluth = "asking" a head of a family of exiles returning with Zerubbabel

H1214.def: : to cut off 1) to cut off, break off, gain by unrighteous violence, get, finish, be covetous, be greedy 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to cut off 1a2) to stop 1a3) to gain wrongfully or by violence 1b) (Piel) 1b1) to cut off, sever 1b2) to finish, complete, accomplish 1b3) to violently make gain of

H1215.def: profit, unjust gain, gain (profit) acquired by violence

H1218.def: § Bozkath or Boscath = "rocky height" a city in Judah in the lowlands toward the Philistines, home of Josiah's mother

H1219.def: 1) to gather, restrain, fence, fortify, make inaccessible, enclose 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to cut off 1a2) fortified, cut off, made inaccessible (pass participle) 1a3) secrets, mysteries, inaccessible things (subst) 1b) (Niphal) to be withheld 1c) (Piel) to fortify

H1221.def: Bezer = "gold ore" or "remote fortress" a city of refuge in Reuben in the downs on the east of the Jordan Also named: bots.rah (בׇּצְרָה "Bozrah" H1224H)

H1224.def: § Bozrah = "sheepfold" or "fortress" a town in Edom

H1227.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezr.2.51 § Bakbuk = "bottle" a head of a family of temple-slaves returning from exile with Zerubbabel

H1229.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Neh.11.17 § Bakbukiah = "wasting of Jehovah" a Levite in the time of Nehemiah

H1230.def: A man of the tribe of Levi living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at 1Ch.9.15 § Bakbakkar = "searcher" a Levite, apparently a descendant of Asaph

H1231.def: A man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, only mentioned at Num.34.22; son of: Jogli (H3020) § Bukki = "wasting" 1) son of Abishua and father of Uzzi, fifth from Aaron in the line of the high priests 2) son of Jogli, prince of the tribe of Dan, one of the ten men chosen to apportion the land of Canaan between the tribes

H1232.def: A man of the tribe of Levi living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 1Ch.25.4; son of: Heman (H1968I); brother of: Mattaniah (H4983H), Uzziel (H5816J), Shebuel (H7619H), Jerimoth (H3406L), Hananiah (H2608P), Hanani (H2607H), Eliathah (H0448), Giddalti (H1437), Romamti-ezer (H7320), Joshbekashah (H3436), Mallothi (H4413), Hothir (H1956), Mahazioth (H4238) § Bukkiah = "Jehovah has emptied" a Kohathite Levite, of the sons of Heman, one of the musicians in the temple

H1234.def: 1) to split, cleave, break open, divide, break through, rip up, break up, tear 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to cleave, cleave open 1a2) to break through, break into 1b) (Niphal) 1b1) to be cleft, be rent open, be split open 1b2) to be broken into 1c) (Piel) 1c1) to cleave, cut to pieces, rend open 1c2) to break through, break down 1d) (Pual) 1d1) to be ripped open, be torn open 1d2) to be rent 1d3) to be broken into 1e) (Hiphil) 1e1) to break into 1e2) to break through 1f) (Hophal) to be broken into 1g) (Hithpael) to burst (themselves) open, cleave asunder

H1235.def: 1) half, part also called "shekel of the sanctuary" or "holy shekel" 2) half a shekel (meton) There appears to be at least three different shekels, one of silver, gold and copper. See a Bible Dictionary for a complete treatment of the subject.

H1237.def: This name means plain, level valley Another name of kik.kar (כִּכָּר "Plain" H3603G)

H1239.def: 1) to seek, enquire, consider 1a) (Piel) 1a1) to seek, look for 1a2) to consider, reflect Aramaic equivalent: be.qar (בְּקַר "to enquire" H1240)

H1240.def: 1) to seek, enquire 1a) (Pael) to enquire 1b) (Ithpael) to let search be made

H1241.def: 1) cattle, herd, oxen, ox 1a) cattle (generic pl. but sing. in form-coll) 1b) herd (particular one) 1c) head of cattle (individually)

H1242.def: 1) morning, break of day 1a) morning 1a1) of end of night 1a2) of coming of daylight 1a3) of coming of sunrise 1a4) of beginning of day 1a5) of bright joy after night of distress (fig.) 1b) morrow, next day, next morning

H1245.def: 1) to seek, require, desire, exact, request 1a) (Piel) 1a1) to seek to find 1a2) to seek to secure 1a3) to seek the face 1a4) to desire, demand 1a5) to require, exact 1a6) to ask, request 1b) (Pual) to be sought

H1247.def: son Aramaic of ben (בֵּן ": child" H1121)

H1248.def: son, heir Another spelling of ben (בֵּן ": child" H1121)

H1249.def: adj 1) pure, clear, sincere 2) clean, empty adv 3) purely

H1253.def: lye, potash, alkali used in smelting metal

H1255.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Ki.20.12; son of: Baladan (H1081) Also named: me.ro.dakh bal.a.dan (מְרֹדַךְ בַּלְאֲדָן "Merodach-baladan" H4757) § Berodach-baladan = "worshipper of Baal" king of Babylon in the days of Hezekiah

H1256.def: A man of the tribe of Benjamin living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.8.21; son of: Shimei (H8096N); brother of: Jakim (H3356), Zichri (H2147H), Zabdi (H2067H), Elienai (H0462), Zillethai (H6769), Eliel (H0447H), Adaiah (H5718H) and Shimrath (H8119) § Beraiah = "Jehovah has created" son of Shimhi, a chief man of Benjamin

H1257.def: 1) (CLBL) fowl, birds 2) (BDB/TWOT) birds fattened for table of Solomon

H1260.def: § Bered = "hail" a place in the south of Palestine, near Kadesh, near the well Lahairoi

H1262.def: 1) to eat, consume 1a) (Qal) to eat 1b) (Piel) for eating, devouring 1c) (Hiphil) to cause to eat

H1263.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Neh.3.20; son of: Zabbai (H2079H) § Baruch = "blessed" 1) friend, amanuensis, and faithful attendant of Jeremiah 2) a priest, the son of Zabbai who assisted Nehemiah in rebuilding the walls of Jerusalem 3) a priest, or family of priests, who signed the covenant with Nehemiah 4) son of Col-hozeh, a descendant of Perez or Pharez, the son of Judah

H1265.def: 1) cypress, fir, juniper, pine 1a) a noble tree (lit.) 1b) of stateliness (fig.) 1c) material for temple

H1269.def: A man of the tribe of Asher living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, only mentioned at 1Ch.7.31; son of: Malchiel (H4439) § Birzavith or Birzath = "in leanness: choice olive" a descendant of Asher

H1270.def: 1) iron 1a) iron 1a1) iron ore 1a2) as material of furniture, utensils, implements 2) tool of iron 3) harshness, strength, oppression (fig.) Aramaic equivalent: par.zel (פַּרְזֶל "iron" H6523)

H1271.def: A man living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.17.27 § Barzillai = "my iron" 1) a Gileadite leader who helped David defeat Absalom's rebellion 2) a priest, son-in-law to Barzillai the Gileadite 3) an Israelite from Mahalath whose son Adriel married Michal, Saul's daughter

H1272.def: 1) to go through, flee, run away, chase, drive away, put to flight, reach, shoot (extend), hurry away 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to go, pass through 1a2) to flee 1a3) to hasten, come quickly 1b) (Hiphil) 1b1) to pass through 1b2) to cause to flee, put to flight 1b3) to drive away

H1273.def: Group of ba.chu.rim (בַּחֻרִים "Bahurim" H0980) § Barhumite = "son of the blackened: in the pitied" a person from Bahurim, a village apparently on or close to the road leading up from the Jordan valley to Jerusalem, and near the south boundary of Benjamin

H1274.def: 1) fat 2) (TWOT) fat, fatter, fed, firm, plenteous, rank

H1275.def: A man of the tribe of Asher living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.7.36; son of: Zophah (H6690); brother of: Suah (H5477), Harnepher (H2774), Shual (H7777B), Imrah (H3236), Bezer (H1221H), Hod (H1936), Shamma (H8037H), Shilshah (H8030), Ithran (H3506H) and Beera (H0878) § Beri = "a well" son of Zophah, of the tribe of Asher

H1276.def: People descended from Beri, a man living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.20.1; father of: Sheba (H7652A) Group of bikh.ri (בִּכְרִי "Bichri" H1075) § Berites = "my well: of the well" a tribe who are named with Abel and Beth-maachah, and who were therefore doubtless situated in the north of Palestine

H1277.def: 1) fat 2) (TWOT) fat, fatter, fed, firm, plenteous, rank Also means: ber.yah (בִּרְיָה "fat" H1274)

H1278.def: a creation, created thing, new thing, marvel

H1280.def: 1) bar 1a) of wood 1b) of city gates 2) of tribulation, a fortress, of the earth as a prison (fig.)

H1282.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.3.22; son of: Shemaiah (H8098B); brother of: Hattush (H2407), Igal (H3008I), Neariah (H5294) and Shaphat (H8202I) § Bariah = "fleeing" or "fugitive" son of Shemaiah and descendant of the royal family of Judah

H1283.def: A man of the tribe of Asher living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Gen.46.17; son of: Asher (H0836); brother of: Imnah (H3232), Ishvah (H3438), Ishvi (H3440) and Serah (H8294); father of: Heber (H2268) and Malchiel (H4439); also called Beriite at Num.26.44; Also named: be.ri.i (בְּרִיעִי "Beriite" H1284) § Beriah = "with a friend" 1) a son of Asher 2) a son of Ephraim 3) a Benjamite 4) a Levite

H1284.def: Someone descended from Beriah who was a man of the tribe of Asher living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, first mentioned at Gen.46.17; son of: Asher (H0836); brother of: Imnah (H3232), Ishvah (H3438), Ishvi (H3440) and Serah (H8294); father of: Heber (H2268) and Malchiel (H4439); also called Beriite at Num.26.44; Group of be.ri.ah (בְּרִיעָה "Beriah" H1283) § Beriites = "in evil" one of the family of Beriah

H1285.def: 1) covenant, alliance, pledge 1a) between men 1a1) treaty, alliance, league (man to man) 1a2) constitution, ordinance (monarch to subjects) 1a3) agreement, pledge (man to man) 1a4) alliance (of friendship) 1a5) alliance (of marriage) 1b) between God and man 1b1) alliance (of friendship) 1b2) covenant (divine ordinance with signs or pledges) 2) (phrases) 2a) covenant making 2b) covenant keeping 2c) covenant violation

H1286.def: Berith = "covenant" in the name of Baal-berith, a foreign deity worshipped in Shechem Combined with el (אֵל "El" H0410H)

H1287.def: lye, potash, soap, alkali (used in washing)

H1288.def: 1) to bless, kneel 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to kneel 1a2) to bless 1b) (Niphal) to be blessed, bless oneself 1c) (Piel) to bless 1d) (Pual) to be blessed, be adored 1e) (Hiphil) to cause to kneel 1f) (Hithpael) to bless oneself 2) (TWOT) to praise, salute, curse Aramaic equivalent: be.rakh (בְּרַךְ "to bless" H1289)

H1289.def: 1) to bless, kneel 1a) (P'al) 1a1) kneeling (participle) 1a2) to be blessed 1b) (Pael) to bless, praise

H1290.def: 1) knee 2) weak from fear (fig.) Aramaic equivalent: be.rekh (בְּרַךְ "knee" H1291)

H1292.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, first mentioned at Job.32.2; father of: Elihu (H0453K) § Barachel = "God blesses" father of Elihu

H1293.def: 1) blessing 2) (source of) blessing 3) blessing, prosperity 4) blessing, praise of God 5) a gift, present 6) treaty of peace

H1294.def: A man living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.12.3 § Berachah = "blessing" a Benjamite, one of David's warriors

H1295.def: pool, pond Combined with she.lach (שֶׁ֫לַח "Shelah" H7975A)

H1296.def: A man of the tribe of Judah living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.3.20; son of: Zerubbabel (H2216); brother of: Meshullam (H4918H), Hananiah (H2608L), Shelomith (H8019I), Hashubah (H2807), Ohel (H0169), Hasadiah (H2619), Jushab-hesed (H3142), Abiud (G0010) § Berachiah or Berechiah = "Jehovah blesses" 1) a son of Zerubbabel 2) a Levite who returned for the exile 3) father of Meshullam, one of Nehemiah's chiefs who assisted in rebuilding the walls of Jerusalem 4) father of Zechariah 5) a Gershonite Levite, the father of Asaph, the singer, doorkeeper of the ark 6) an Ephraimite chief in the time of Ahaz

H1298.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, only mentioned at Gen.14.2 § Bera = "son of evil" a king of Sodom

H1301.def: A man living before Israel's Monarchy, first mentioned at Jdg.4.6; son of: Abinoam (H0042) § Barak = "lightning" or "lightning flash" son of Abinoam of Kedesh who, incited by Deborah, a prophetess of Ephraim, delivered the Israelites from the yoke of Jabin by routing the Canaanites in the plain of Jezreel

H1302.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, first mentioned at Ezr.2.53 § Barkos = "the son cut off" head of a family of temple-slaves who returned from exile with Zerubbabel

H1305.def: 1) to purify, select, polish, choose, purge, cleanse or make bright, test or prove 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to purge, purge out, purify 1a2) to choose, select 1a3) to cleanse, make shining, polish 1a4) to test, prove 1b) (Niphal) to purify oneself 1c) (Piel) to purify 1d) (Hiphil) 1d1) to purify 1d2) to polish arrows 1e) (Hithpael) 1e1) to purify oneself 1e2) to show oneself pure, just, kind

H1306.def: A man living at the time of the Patriarchs, only mentioned at Gen.14.2 § Birsha = "with iniquity" a king of Gomorrah

H1307.def: Berothite = "my wells" an inhabitant of Berothah Another spelling of be.e.rot (בְּאֵרוֹת "Beeroth" H0881)

H1308.def: § Besor = "cheerful" a stream, torrent-bed, or wadi in extreme south of Judah in Philistia; empties into the Mediterranean Sea

H1309.def: 1) news, good news, tidings, reward for good news 1a) good tidings 1b) tidings, news 1c) reward for good tidings

H1310.def: 1) to boil, cook, bake, roast, ripen, grow ripe 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to boil, cook 1a2) to grow ripe, ripen 1b) (Piel) 1b1) to boil 1b2) to cook 1c) (Pual) 1c1) to be boiled 1c2) to be sodden 1d) (Hiphil) 1d1) to ripen 1d2) ripen, brought to ripeness

H1312.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Ezr.4.7 § Bishlam = "in peace" a Persian officer in Palestine in the time of Ezra

H1313.def: 1) spice, balsam 2) (TWOT) sweet, sweet smell, sweet odour

H1314.def: 1) spice, balsam, balsam tree, perfume 2) (TWOT) sweet, sweet smell, sweet odour

H1315.def: A woman of the tribe of Judah living at the time of United Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ki.4.15; daughter of: Solomon (H8010); sister of: Taphath (H2955) and Rehoboam (H7346); married to Ahimaaz (H0290I) § Bashemath or Basmath = "spice" 1) Hittite wife of Esau, daughter of Ishmael, sister of Nebaioth 2) daughter of Solomon, wife of Ahimaaz, one of Solomon's commissariat officers

H1316.def: § Bashan = "fruitful" a district east of the Jordan known for its fertility which was given to the half-tribe of Manasseh

H1319.def: 1) to bear news, bear tidings, publish, preach, show forth 1a) (Piel) 1a1) to gladden with good news 1a2) to bear news 1a3) to announce (salvation) as good news, preach 1b) (Hithpael) to receive good news

H1320.def: 1) flesh 1a) of the body 1a1) of humans 1a2) of animals 1b) the body itself 1c) male organ of generation (euphemism) 1d) kindred, blood-relations 1e) flesh as frail or erring (man against God) 1f) all living things 1g) animals 1h) mankind Aramaic equivalent: be.shar (בְּשַׁר "flesh" H1321)

H1323.def: : daughter 1) daughter 1a) daughter, girl, adopted daughter, daughter-in-law, sister, granddaughters, female child, cousin 1a1) as polite address 1a2) as designation of women of a particular place 2) young women, women 1a3) as personification 1a4) daughter-villages 1a5) description of character Also means: bat (בַּת ": village" H1323H)

H1324.def: bath, a unit of liquid measure, equal to dry measure ephah (about 9 imperial gallons or 40 litres, rabbinical writings give sizes of one-half this amount) Aramaic equivalent: bat (בַּת "bath" H1325)

H1325.def: 1) bath, a unit of liquid measure, about 40 litres, equal to dry measure ephah 2) (TWOT) a liquid measure, about 22 litres

H1329.def: § Bethul = "God destroys" or "man of God" or "dweller in God" a town of Simeon in the south (also spelled 'Bethuel')

H1332.def: A woman of the Egyptians living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.4.18; daughter of: Pharaoh (H6547M); married to Mered (H4778)(?); mother of: Miriam (H4813H), Shammai (H8060I) and Ishbah (H3431) § Bithiah = "daughter of Jehovah" a daughter of a Pharaoh and wife of Mered of Judah

H1333.def: (Piel) to cut, cut up, cut off, cut down

H1334.def: 1) to cut in two 1a) (Qal) to cut in two 1b) (Piel) to cut in two

H1335.def: 1) part, piece 1a) of the parts of an animal cut in half for a sacrifice

H1336.def: § Bether = "cleft" a mountainous region in Palestine

H1337.def: § Bath-rabbim = "daughter of multitudes" the city of Heshbon or one of its gates

H1338.def: § Bithron = "division" or "cleft" or "ravine" a territory or district in the Jordan valley east of the Jordan

H1339.def: A woman living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.11.3; daughter of: Eliam (H0463); married to David (H1732) and Uriah (H0223A); mother of: Shammua (H8051H), Shobab (H7727), Nathan (H5416) and Solomon (H8010); also called Bath-shua at 1Ch.3.5; Also named: bat-shu.a (בַּת־שׁוּעַ "Bath-shua" H1340) § Bath-sheba = "daughter of an oath" the wife of Uriah whom David had murdered, having had adulterous relations with her; subsequently wife of David and mother of Solomon, Shimea, Shobab, and Nathan

H1340.def: A woman living at the time of United Monarchy, first mentioned at 2Sa.11.3; daughter of: Eliam (H0463); married to David (H1732) and Uriah (H0223A); mother of: Shammua (H8051H), Shobab (H7727), Nathan (H5416) and Solomon (H8010); also called Bath-shua at 1Ch.3.5; Another spelling of bat-she.va (בַּת־שֶׁ֫בַע "Bathsheba" H1339) § Bath-shua = "daughter of wealth" 1) the wife of Uriah whom David had murdered, having had adulterous relations with her; subsequently wife of David and mother of Solomon, Shimea, Shobab, and Nathan (alternate spelling to 'Bathsheba') 2) wife of Judah

H1342.def: 1) to rise up, grow up, be exalted in triumph 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to rise up 1a2) to grow up 1a3) to be lifted up, be raised up, be exalted

H1345.def: A man living at the time of Egypt and Wilderness, only mentioned at Num.13.15; son of: Machi (H4352) § Geuel = "majesty of God" the Gadite chosen to spy out the land, son of Machi

H1346.def: 1) pride, majesty, a rising up 1a) a rising up, swelling (of the sea) 1b) majesty (of Israel) 1c) pride, haughtiness

H1347.def: 1) exaltation, majesty, pride 1a) majesty, exaltation, excellence 1a1) of nations 1a2) of God 1a3) of the Jordan 1b) pride, arrogance (bad sense)

H1348.def: 1) majesty 1a) a rising up (of column of smoke) 1b) a swelling (of sea) 1c) majesty (of God) 1d) pride

H1351.def: 1) to defile, pollute, desecrate 1a) (Niphal) to be defiled, be polluted 1b) (Piel) to pollute, desecrate 1c)(Pual) to be desecrated (of removal from priesthood) 1d) (Hiphil) to pollute, stain 1e) (Hithpael) to defile oneself

H1353.def: 1) kindred, redemption, right of redemption, price of redemption 1a) kin, kindred 1b) redemption 1c) right of redemption 1d) price of redemption, redemption price

H1354.def: 1) convex surface, back 1a) back (of man) 1b) mound (for illicit worship) 1c) boss (convex projection of shield) 1d) bulwarks, breastworks (of arguments-fig.) 1e) brow, eyebrow 1f) rim (of wheel) Aramaic equivalent: gav (גַּב "back" H1355)

H1355.def: back or side Aramaic of gav (גַּב "back/rim/brow" H1354)

H1359.def: Gob = "cistern" a place which was the scene of two encounters between David's warriors and the Philistines (also '"Gezer') Another name of ge.zer (גֶּ֫זֶר "Gezer" H1507)

H1361.def: 1) to be high, be exalted 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to be high, lofty, tall 1a2) to be exalted 1a3) to be lofty 1a3a) to be lofty (of Jehovah's ways-good sense) 1a3b) to be haughty, be arrogant (bad sense) 1b) (Hiphil) to make high, exalt

H1362.def: 1) high, proud 1a) high, lofty, tall 1b) exalted in station 1c) haughty, proud

H1363.def: 1) height, exaltation 1a) height 1b) exaltation, grandeur 1c) haughtiness

H1364.def: adj 1) high, exalted 1a) high, tall 1b) high (in position) 1c) proud, haughty n m 2) loftiness Also means: ga.vah (גָּבֹהַּ "high" H1362)

H1366.def: : boundary 1) border, territory 1a) border 1b) territory (enclosed within boundary) 1c) region, territory (of darkness) (fig.) Also means: ge.vul (גְּבוּל ": area" H1366H)

H1368.def: adj 1) strong, mighty n m 2) strong man, brave man, mighty man

H1369.def: 1) strength, might 1a) strength 1b) might, valour, bravery 1c) might, mighty deeds (of God) Aramaic equivalent: ge.vu.rah (גְּבוּרָה "might" H1370)

H1371.def: to be high, bald (in the forehead), having a bald forehead

H1372.def: bald forehead, bald, a bare spot

H1373.def: A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Neh.11.8; also translated "men"; § Gabbai = "my back" the head of an important Benjamite family living in Jerusalem in the time of Nehemiah

H1374.def: § Gebim = "cisterns" a village north of Jerusalem apparently between Anathoth (modern Anata) and the ridge on which Nob was situated

H1379.def: 1) to bound, border 1a) (Qal) to bound, border 1b) (Hiphil) to set bounds, set bounds for

H1380.def: § Gebal = "mountain" a maritime town of Phoenicia near Tyre (modern 'Jebeil') known to the Greeks as 'Byblus'
